title: Make Medication Review Prescriber use objective miss-count threshold
description:
Replace the current SRX "most medications" prescriber standard with the same kind of objective threshold used by Medication Review: Dosage.

Scope:
- Update Medication Review: Prescriber score code/prompt in Plexus
- Update official guidelines to match
- Keep SPM PCP-only logic unchanged
- Publish one merged version containing both code and guidelines
- Promote that merged version to champion
- Rerun fresh 180-day FeedbackContradictions for Prescriber and summarize the delta

Out of scope:
- legacy Medication Review
- other Medication Review split scores unless required by direct dependency

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Confirmed the live Prescriber champion still uses a subjective SRX "most medications" standard. Also found a later non-champion draft (`f20ca228-acef-4475-90bc-345ec877c43a`) that reintroduced `parse_from_start: true`, so the new objective-threshold version will be built intentionally from the correct parse-from-end state rather than inheriting that regression.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Created merged Prescriber version `27816b39-0bb8-4fd8-adec-934d40cb47ab` from champion `edc3048a-6e2c-455c-8937-c635b0c02525`, replacing the SRX subjective \"most medications\" standard with an explicit miss-count threshold in both the guidelines and the classifier prompt. Next step is exact-version verification, quick score execution smoke test, then champion promotion and a fresh contradictions rerun.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Version-specific prediction lookup continued to resolve the old champion, so I promoted the verified merged version first rather than trusting the pre-promotion smoke test. Next step is post-promotion prediction confirmation and a fresh 180-day contradictions rerun against the new champion.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Completed the Prescriber objective-threshold update.

Published/promoted version:
- `Medication Review: Prescriber` -> `27816b39-0bb8-4fd8-adec-934d40cb47ab`

What changed:
- Replaced the SRX subjective "most medications" rubric with an explicit threshold: `No` when two or more current medications are missing prescriber verification; `Yes` when no more than one current medication is missing prescriber verification.
- Updated both the official guidelines and the live classifier prompt/code in the same version.
- Preserved the SPM PCP-only rule.
- Preserved parse-from-end behavior and avoided inheriting the later non-champion `parse_from_start` regression.
- Added explicit SRX handling that unresolved patient uncertainty on a current medication counts as a miss, and that chart-led list-correctness confirmation does not by itself verify the prescriber.

Verification:
- Validated the rewritten guidelines format before publishing.
- Verified the created version contains both the updated code and the updated guidelines.
- Promoted the merged version to champion.
- Post-promotion prediction smoke tests executed successfully on:
  - SRX-like item `9c929f25-a91f-4db7-8943-5aa93498b8e9--309504474`
  - SPM-like item `9c929f25-a91f-4db7-8943-5aa93498b8e9--305879339`

Fresh 180-day contradiction rerun after promotion:
- Before: 13 contradictions / 41 aligned
- After: 10 contradictions / 44 aligned

Remaining contradiction themes:
- Older feedback applying an all-meds standard or treating a single missed prescriber as `No` (now clearer invalidation-review candidates).
- SPM calls still being judged with SRX-style per-medication expectations.
- Two residual low-confidence policy-gap buckets: one agent-uncertainty wording edge case and one transcription-artifact / `prescribed by` detection item.

This work narrows the contradiction set and makes the Prescriber rubric consistent with the same kind of objective threshold already used on Dosage.
